name: Mativ Holdings, Inc.
business: Mativ is a global leader in specialty materials, manufacturing highly engineered media such as nonwovens, films, specialty paper, and netting for demanding applications. The company operates through two segments: Filtration & Advanced Materials (FAM) and Sustainable & Adhesive Solutions (SAS). FAM sells filtration media, advanced films, and extruded mesh to system integrators and downstream manufacturers; SAS sells tapes, labels, liners, specialty paper, packaging, and healthcare solutions through distributors, converters, retailers, and direct channels.

exposures.sentence: Filtration & netting - includes specialty filtration media and components aiding in the separation and purification of air and liquids for a variety of applications, such as transportation applications, water filtration, industrial processes, life science, HVAC and air pollution control.
Healthcare & other includes advanced wound care, consumer wellness, device fixation, medical packaging, as well as a wide range of other solutions and applications.

Our tapes and industrial products provide solutions used in growing areas such as cable harnesses and tape for electric vehicles, and protective coverings for cables used in infrastructure and construction.
Our advanced films are used in automotive and building applications to safeguard surfaces from sunlight, sound, impact, water, and temperature increasingly relevant to protect vehicles and buildings.

Paper & packaging includes premium printing and other specialty papers and packaging applications used for print collateral, advertising, direct mail, sustainable alternatives, product packaging, graphics, wallpaper and education, as well as consumer office, stationery and craft papers sold to large retailers.
The segment also includes extruded mesh products used in agriculture and various packaging applications.

catalysts.event: Plan savings target: additional $15-20 million cost reductions by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: The next wave of initiatives will target additional cost reductions of $15.0 million to $20.0 million by the end of 2026.
On February 18, 2026, we announced a cash dividend of $0.10 per share payable on March 27, 2026, to stockholders of record as of the close of business on March 13, 2026.

catalysts.event: Tentative quarterly earnings call dates for 2026 results
catalysts.sentence: The tentative dates for our quarterly earnings conference calls related to 2026 financial results are May 7, 2026, August 6, 2026, November 5, 2026, and February 18, 2027.
